title: Coordinates, Scalars, Vector2, Angles, and Units · Language Clinic: Zero Vector
summary: The ship stores integer x and y, mixes degrees with radians, normalizes a zero vector, treats screen y as mathematical y, and adds pixels per second directly to position. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the zero vector increment in unit-labeled 2D geometry model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice,…
objectives: Guard normalization and scale-to-length operations when a vector has no direction.
Derive facing vectors from a named zero-angle and degree convention and test clockwise screen appearance.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: asteroids-zero-vector — Guard normalization and scale-to-length operations when a vector has no direction.
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-coordinate-vector-units-3
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-coordinate-vector-units-3.
# Case 5798: a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial.
# Operating constraint: retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity.
# Changed case: cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity.
def worked_asteroids_coordinate_vector_units_3(x: float = 3.0, y: float = 4.0, pixels_per_second: float = 120.0, seconds: float = 0.25, zero_normalized: bool = False) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "playerworked3",
        "seed5798",
        "input3dekrow",
        "frameworked35798",
        "state5798worked3",
        "playtest3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    magnitude = (x * x + y * y) ** 0.5
    if magnitude <= 0.0:
        return False, magnitude, "zero-vector"
    if pixels_per_second < 0.0 or seconds < 0.0:
        return False, seconds, "unit-domain"
    if zero_normalized:
        return False, magnitude, "zero-normalization"
    displacement = pixels_per_second * seconds
    assert abs(magnitude - 5.0) < 1e-9
    return True, displacement, "explicit-units"

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-zero-vector-1gnbfp
# Competency: asteroids-zero-vector.
# Case 6899: a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling.
# Operating constraint: test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases.
# Changed case: cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity.
def evidence_asteroids_zero_vector_1gnbfp(x: float = 3.0, y: float = 4.0, pixels_per_second: float = 120.0, seconds: float = 0.25, zero_normalized: bool = False) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1gnbfp = "1gnbfp-pfbng1-6899"
    assert evidence_variant_1gnbfp
    evidence_axes_1gnbfp = (
        "player1gnbfp",
        "seed6899",
        "inputpfbng1",
        "frame1gnbfp6899",
        "state68991gnbfp",
        "playtestpfbng16899",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1gnbfp)) == 6
    magnitude = (x * x + y * y) ** 0.5
    if magnitude <= 0.0:
        return False, magnitude, "zero-vector"
    if pixels_per_second < 0.0 or seconds < 0.0:
        return False, seconds, "unit-domain"
    if zero_normalized:
        return False, magnitude, "zero-normalization"
    displacement = pixels_per_second * seconds
    assert abs(magnitude - 5.0) < 1e-9
    return True, displacement, "explicit-units"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Guard normalization and scale-to-length operations when a vector has no direction.

hints: For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Zero Vector model · Theory Zero Vector, name the player, revision, runtime, seed, input, time, coordinate, state, entity, collision, render, cue, accessibility, package, release, and desktop-transfer boundary, Zero Vector precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Zero Vector model · Theory Zero Vector, trace this concrete input and predict event, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, cue, accessibility, failure, pause, rollback, recovery, and desktop evidence before reviewing it: A theory Asteroids team handles case 6440 during a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile screen coordinates, Vector2 magnitude and direction, angle convention, and explicit motion units; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then lose window focus while thrust and fire are held, then prove both actions release safely.
